title: ADRAFINIL
description: Adrafinil is a eugeroic compound and prodrug to modafinil. It promotes wakefulness and alertness without the jittery effects of traditional stimulants. Metabolized in the liver to modafinil, it offers similar cognitive benefits.

research.title: Behavioral Activating Effects of Adrafinil in Aged Canines
research.summary: Study demonstrates that adrafinil increases locomotor activity and directed sniffing in aged dogs, with variable individual responses.

research.title: Identification of adrafinil and its main metabolite modafinil in human hair
research.summary: Research shows adrafinil and modafinil can be detected in human hair following single consumption, helping distinguish trafficking cases from self-use.

research.title: Adrafinil disrupts performance on a delayed nonmatching-to-position task in aged beagle dogs
research.summary: Study reveals adrafinil significantly impairs working memory in aged dogs, potentially via increased noradrenergic transmission in prefrontal cortex.

research.title: Adrafinil: Effects on behavior and cognition in aged canines
research.summary: Research characterizes adrafinil as a behavioral stimulant with cognitive enhancement potential in aged dogs, though mechanisms remain unclear.

research.title: Oral Administration of Adrafinil Improves Discrimination Learning in Aged Beagle Dogs
research.summary: Investigation demonstrates adrafinil significantly enhances discrimination learning in aged dogs, possibly through enhanced vigilance and motivation.

research.title: Fluorescent zinc and copper complexes for detection of adrafinil
research.summary: Fluorescent complexes provide detection method for adrafinil in paper-based microfluidic devices, useful for identifying banned performance-enhancing substances.

research.title: Thermal degradation of modafinil analogs in GC injector
research.summary: Analysis shows thermal degradation of modafinil and adrafinil compounds in gas chromatography injectors can produce unexpected byproducts.

research.title: Comparison of the effects of adrafinil, propentofylline, and nicergoline on behavior in aged dogs
research.summary: Research indicates adrafinil treatment may improve quality of life in aged dogs by increasing exploratory behavior and alertness.

research.title: Development and Validation of an Analytical Method to Identify Novel Modafinil Analogs
research.summary: LC-HRMS method effectively identifies and quantifies novel modafinil analogs in dietary supplements, protecting consumers from undisclosed active ingredients.

sentiment.summary: Users appreciate its wakefulness-promoting effects, though many prefer modafinil directly due to concerns about liver metabolism.

effects.label: Individual Variation:
effects.description: Response varies based on liver enzyme activity and metabolism.

effects.label: Tolerance Development:
effects.description: Some users report diminishing effects with regular use.

dosage.label: Typical Range:
dosage.description: 300-600mg per day, usually taken in the morning.

dosage.label: Avoid Daily Use:
dosage.description: Due to liver metabolism concerns, many cycle usage or use intermittently.

sideEffects.label: Liver Strain:
sideEffects.description: Long-term use may stress the liver; liver function monitoring is recommended.

sideEffects.label: Headaches:
sideEffects.description: Some users report headaches, often mitigated by staying hydrated.

sideEffects.label: Insomnia:
sideEffects.description: Taking too late in the day can disrupt sleep.

availability.label: Unscheduled:
availability.description: Legal in most countries without prescription.
